domain_experience: deep understanding of where provider groups lose money on denials and prior auth
assets: operational understanding of denial and prior-auth failure modes
ability to convert payer and workflow complexity into product logic
distribution: best-fit channels are self-serve landing pages, written content, SEO, and async email distribution
partnership distribution through non-customer RCM consultants or billing services may be viable
network_access: possible access to adjacent non-customer buyers such as RCM consultants, billing services, MSOs, and independent provider operators
constraints: does not want consulting or sales calls
credibility_signals: staff engineer at a healthcare claims startup
credible domain fluency in denial and prior-auth operations
unfair_advantages: superior problem selection in a narrow high-value workflow
better than generic AI founders at knowing which payer and denial issues matter
can build products that are operationally useful without relying on regulated data

opportunities.title: Async denial leakage memo for non-overlapping buyers
opportunities.thesis: Sell a fixed-scope asynchronous review that uses public payer policies, generalized expertise, and client-provided de-identified samples to identify likely denial and prior-auth leakage points and recommend corrective actions.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user already knows which workflow failures matter and can produce a sharper memo than a generic consultant or AI agency.
validation_plan: Create a sample memo from public sources and synthetic examples
Offer a fixed-scope async review with no meetings
Test demand with a landing page and a small amount of written outbound
kill_criteria: buyers insist on meetings or custom consulting
buyers require PHI or internal data for the offer to be useful

opportunities.title: Denial and prior-auth playbook library with recurring updates
opportunities.thesis: Build a subscription library of denial root-cause playbooks, appeal frameworks, prior-auth checklists, and AI prompts using only public rules, synthetic scenarios, and generalized expertise.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user can make the content operationally precise and valuable, not generic healthcare AI content.
validation_plan: Publish 10 sample playbooks for one specialty
Test paid interest with a simple subscription page
Measure whether users value recurring updates over one-time templates
kill_criteria: users treat it as free-content material rather than a paid operating tool
recurring updates do not materially increase willingness to pay

opportunities.title: Synthetic denial benchmark and training dataset
opportunities.thesis: Create a synthetic dataset of denial and prior-auth scenarios with labeled causes, actions, and resolution paths for QA, training, and workflow testing.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user knows which scenarios are realistic, costly, and worth encoding into a structured asset.
validation_plan: Test interest from adjacent buyers like consultants or vendors
Confirm whether dataset buyers exist before scaling the asset
kill_criteria: prospects prefer services or software over datasets

opportunities.title: Payer policy diff monitor to action playbooks to self-serve copilot
opportunities.thesis: Start with a subscription that monitors public payer policy and prior-auth changes for a single specialty, translates changes into operational actions, and later adds a copilot that maps issues to likely causes, next steps, and appeal checklist templates.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user can convert noisy payer changes into operationally useful guidance because they understand exactly where provider groups lose money and what workflows break next.
validation_plan: Pick one specialty and payer cluster
Create 3 sample outputs from public sources only
Launch a landing page with screenshots and a self-serve CTA
Run 2 weeks of written distribution and measure signups or preorders
tool_specs: searchable knowledge base and simple copilot UI
kill_criteria: public policy changes are too infrequent or too low-signal to support recurring value
2 weeks of distribution produce no signups, replies, or willingness to pay
the product requires PHI or customer-specific data to be useful

opportunities.title: White-label copilot for RCM consultants and billing services
opportunities.thesis: Package denial and prior-auth intelligence into a branded tool and template bundle for independent RCM consultants and billing services so they can serve their clients faster without requiring the user to join calls.
opportunities.why_user_can_win: The user provides the specialized intelligence layer while adjacent experts provide the buyer relationships.
validation_plan: Email a small set of non-customer consultants with the artifact
Test whether channel partners want recurring access
kill_criteria: partner acquisition is too slow without calls
consultants prefer custom documents over a reusable product

next_actions: Review employment, moonlighting, confidentiality, and invention-assignment obligations before launch
Choose one specialty and one payer cluster with frequent prior-auth or denial changes
Produce 3 sample artifacts using only public sources and synthetic scenarios
Launch a one-page site with a self-serve waitlist or preorder CTA
Run 2 weeks of written distribution through posts, articles, and async email
Continue only if there is evidence of recurring demand for the subscription format
